2026-03-10.title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम बंगाल में चुनावी मतदाता सूची विवाद: सुप्रीम कोर्ट के आदेश की व्याख्या
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Election Commission and Electoral Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court's order comes amidst 60 lakh pending cases in West Bengal. The Election Commission's current system for resolving electoral roll disputes has been called into question. The publication of final rolls has significant implications for the state's electoral process. The Supreme Court's decision aims to address the backlog of cases and ensure a more efficient dispute resolution mechanism. The move may have far-reaching consequences for the Election Commission's structure and functioning.
key_points: The Supreme Court — Judicial Officers Deployment: Ordered deployment to decide electoral roll disputes in West Bengal.
Election Commission — Staffing: Debate triggered on equipping with permanent staff to manage electoral roll disputes.
West Bengal Government — Electoral Roll Disputes: 60 lakh cases pending adjudication, sparking concern over electoral process.
The Supreme Court — Order: Aims to address backlog of cases and ensure efficient dispute resolution mechanism.
Election Commission — Structure: Potential implications for the Commission's structure and functioning in light of the Supreme Court's order.

2026-03-10.title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधान मंत्री ₹10,800 करोड़ की परियोजनाओं का शुभारंभ करेंगे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister will launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at an event in Kochi, aiming to boost infrastructure development in the region. The event will include the laying of the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit at the Kochi Refinery, as well as the inauguration of 23 rural roads constructed under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana. This initiative is expected to improve connectivity and economic growth in the area. The projects are part of the government's efforts to enhance the country's infrastructure and promote rural development. The total investment of ₹10,800 crore is a significant step towards achieving these goals.
key_points: The Prime Minister — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Inaugurating 23 rural roads (₹X crore).
The Prime Minister — Kochi Refinery: Laying the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit (₹X crore).
The Government — Infrastructure Development: Investing ₹10,800 crore in projects.
The Prime Minister — Rural Development: Aiming to improve connectivity and economic growth.
The Government — Economic Growth: Expecting a significant boost to the local economy.

2026-03-10.title: Supreme Court approves Centre's proposal extending term of tribunal members
2026-03-10.title_hi: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने केंद्र के प्रस्ताव को मंजूरी दी, ट्रिब्यूनल सदस्यों का कार्यकाल बढ़ाया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Judiciary and Tribunal Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court has approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021. About 21 members are due to retire in the meantime. A new law is likely to be enacted by next September, either in this Budget session or the Monsoon session. The Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 will continue to apply to everybody who was appointed under it. The Attorney General R Venkataramani stated that the new law will be enacted soon.
key_points: Supreme Court — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members.
Centre — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Proposed to extend the term of about 21 members due to retire.
Attorney General R Venkataramani — New Law: Stated that a new law is likely to be enacted by next September.
Supreme Court — Tribunal Members: Extended the term of members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021.
Centre — New Law: Likely to enact a new law in either the Budget session or the Monsoon session.

2026-03-10.title: We will have to approach court against Centre to seek justice on devolution of funds, says CM Siddaramaiah
2026-03-10.title_hi: कर्नाटक को न्याय दिलाने के लिए केंद्र के खिलाफ कोर्ट जाना पड़ सकता है: सिद्धारमैया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Interstate Relations and Centre-State Relations
2026-03-10.context: The Karnataka government is considering legal action against the Centre over the devolution of funds. The state had previously received ₹3,000 crore in drought relief assistance from the Centre after approaching the Supreme Court. The Chief Minister, Siddaramaiah, highlighted this instance as a precedent for potential future action. The devolution of funds is a critical issue for the state, with significant implications for its finances and development projects. The Centre's allocation of funds to states is based on various criteria, including population, area, and revenue generation.
key_points: The Karnataka government — Drought Relief Assistance: Received ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after approaching the Supreme Court.
The Centre — Finance Commission Recommendations: Implemented the 14th Finance Commission's recommendations from 2015-16, increasing the share of states in tax revenue.
The Karnataka government — Devolution of Funds: Considering legal action against the Centre over the issue.
The Supreme Court — Previous Interventions: Has provided guidance on the distribution of funds between the Centre and states in the past.
The Centre — Fund Allocation: Allocates funds to states based on criteria such as population, area, and revenue generation.

2026-03-10.title: UP govt signs pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity
2026-03-10.title_hi: उत्तर प्रदेश सरकार ने हिंदुजा समूह की कंपनी के साथ ब्रॉडबैंड कनेक्टिविटी विस्तार के लिए समझौता किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Uttar Pradesh government has signed a pact with a Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity in the state. The pact aims to provide high-speed internet services to around 3,50,000 villages and 20,000 government institutions. The project is expected to be completed within the next 2 years at a cost of ₹2,500 crore. The Hinduja Group firm will be responsible for designing, building, and operating the broadband network. The project will also create employment opportunities for around 10,000 people.
key_points: UP Government — Broadband Expansion: Providing high-speed internet services to 3,50,000 villages and 20,000 government institutions.
Hinduja Group firm — Project Implementation: Designing, building, and operating the broadband network at a cost of ₹2,500 crore.
UP Government — Employment Generation: Creating employment opportunities for around 10,000 people.
Centre — BharatNet Initiative: Aiming to provide high-speed internet services to all gram panchayats in the country by 2025.
UP Government — Project Timeline: Completing the project within the next 2 years.
